sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious Copy From or To System Directory
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects a suspicious copy operation that tries to copy a program from system (System32, SysWOW64, WinSxS) directories to another on disk.
Often used to move LOLBINs such as 'certutil' or 'desktopimgdownldr' to a different location with a different name in order to bypass detections based on locations.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Markus Neis, Tim Shelton (HAWK.IO), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

values.CommandLine: C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe" /c vssadmin delete shadows /all /quiet & wmic shadowcopy delete & bcdedit /set {default} bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures & bcdedit /set {default} recoveryenabled no & wbadmin delete catalog -quiet

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: This is a LockBit ransomware sample. The binary enumerates local and network drives to encrypt user files, skipping critical system directories and specific file extensions. Before encryption, it attempts to terminate a long list of processes and services (including those for databases, backups, and security software) to ensure files are unlocked. It actively disables system recovery by deleting volume shadow copies using `vssadmin.exe`, `wmic.exe`, and `bcdedit.exe`. The malware creates the registry key `SOFTWARE\LockBit` to store cryptographic information and drops a ransom note named `Restore-My-Files.txt` containing the text "All your important files are encrypted!". It also attempts to download a configuration file from `https://iplo.ru/1LJjq7.txt` and uses a UAC bypass technique involving the `ICM\Calibration` registry key.
